My kitchen MVP lately? My @kuvings_sa nutriblender ￼ making smoothies in minutes, and my reusable @ecoffeecup.sa keeping mom life a little easier on the go ☕️ Balanced, realistic nutrition for real life.
No perfection. Just small nourishing habits that fit into busy days.
Wie sê gesond eet moet complicated wees? 🇿🇦✨
:
Strawberry Chocolate Protein Smoothie 🍓🍫
Ingredients
* 1 cup frozen strawberries
* 1 small banana
* 1 tbsp cocoa powder
* 1 scoop chocolate protein powder
Additionally for extra nutrition and satiety 
* 1 tbsp peanut butter or almond butter
* 1 tbsp oats
* 1 tsp chia seeds
* 1 cup milk of choice
* Handful ice
* Optional: espresso shot for a mocha twist ☕️
Method
1. Add everything to your  Kuvings South Africa￼ blender and blend until smooth and creamy.
2. Pour into your reusable  Ecoffee Cup South Africa￼ cup and head out the door ✨

Reuse only works if the product lasts and Ecoffee Cup® is built for daily life. From busy mornings and school runs to drinks on the go and long meetings: it’s light, durable, and made to be used again and again.

The classic cup is crafted from pure, single-source PLA (polylactic acid) derived from sugar beet - completely free from BPA, BPS, melamine, and bamboo. Its lid and sleeve are made from food-grade silicone, free from natural rubber latex. A sustainable cup that’s as hardworking as you are.

Lunch break coffee ;) Why should I choose to re-use? 🌿
❗️Each year over 300 billion single-use coffee cups are manufactured globally.❗️Due to their plastic content, less than 2% will ever be recycled. As takeaway drink consumption grows, so does the problem of waste.

Each time you choose to re-use, even just once a week, you will be helping in the fight against single-use waste. Imagine the massive difference we could make if everyone did the same. If 2 million people chose to re-use just once a week, we would stop 104,000,000 single-use cups going to landfill each year! 🌍

Our range of #proudlysouthafrican Wanderland Ecoffee Cups features four #local #female artists’ work from the @wanderland_collective. Faatimah Mohamed-Luke’s “Mother Tongue” Ecoffee Cup design is inspired by the knowledge we learn outside of formal education - knowledge taught through storytelling and narratives passed through lineages over centuries, present around us in everyday items and ceremonial traditions - expressed as a visual language of patterns and symbolism. 
.
“Within these pieces, the symbols have been deconstructed so that we may relearn and examine the meanings and messages behind each symbol and colour combination. Within the pattern and between the pattern, I have created an openness and open-mindedness between our cultures. My hope is that in sharing and learning our newfound collective knowledge, we will create a rich and more accurate global history. This understanding of the past and present should ultimately create admiration and respect for our uniqueness.” @lucky_lady_luke
.

“Being a native Joburger, December holidays were always spent alongside the Cape coast where the exotic allure of the ocean took hold. This coupled with endless hours watching the whimsical underwater worlds of ‘Planet Earth’, catalysed the desire to capture this weird and wonderful space on fabric. Curling, twirling shells, textured mosses, dancing algae and darting jellyfish submerge us into this magical aquatic world.” 
.
Today we feature the gorgeous and wildly #popular Oceanum Ecoffee Cup design by #local textile designer Nicole Levenberg, @aurumdesign in collaboration with the @wanderland_collective – an initiative supporting #proudlysouthafrican #female artists.
.
